steps: Go to https://cloud.hasura.io and sign up or log in
Click 'New Project' to create a new Hasura Cloud project, or select an existing project
Once the project is created, click the settings (gear) icon on the project card
Go to the 'Env vars' tab in project settings
Find the 'HASURA_GRAPHQL_ADMIN_SECRET' environment variable — this is your API key
Click the eye icon to reveal the admin secret value and copy it
Use this value as the 'x-hasura-admin-secret' header in your API requests

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Admin Secret
credential_fields.description: Your Hasura project's admin secret, sent in the 'x-hasura-admin-secret' request header

notes: Hasura Cloud Free tier available; paid plans (Professional, Enterprise) for production workloads
The admin secret is auto-generated at project creation but can be changed in project settings
Admin secret bypasses all row-level security and permission rules — keep it strictly server-side
For self-hosted Hasura, set HASURA_GRAPHQL_ADMIN_SECRET as an environment variable on startup
The base_url must be replaced with your actual Hasura project URL (e.g., https://your-project.hasura.app)

test_endpoint.description: Check the Hasura instance health to verify the server is reachable
